title: Listen and Choose a Response — Everyday Situations

questions.stem: Sorry I'm late. The bus broke down and I had to wait for the next one.
questions.explanation: The speaker is apologizing and explaining a delay, so graciously accepting the apology and moving on is appropriate.

questions.stem: Do you have a pen I could borrow? Mine just ran out of ink.

options.C: You should buy a new one.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking to borrow an item, so a direct offer to lend is the expected response.

questions.stem: I have three exams next week and I'm really stressed about it.

options.A: You should have started studying earlier.
options.B: I know how you feel. Let's make a study plan together.
options.C: Exams are only worth half the grade.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing stress, so an empathetic response and offer to help is more supportive than criticism.

questions.stem: So, you're saying the deadline was moved to Thursday instead of Friday?

options.A: That's correct. We have one extra day.

questions.explanation: The speaker is checking their understanding of information, so confirming or correcting the details is appropriate.

questions.stem: Your presentation was incredible! How did you prepare so well?

options.A: Thank you! I practiced in front of a mirror every day.
options.B: Presentations are required for this class.
options.C: Yours was pretty good too, I guess.

questions.explanation: The speaker is giving a compliment, so graciously accepting it and answering the follow-up question is polite and natural.

questions.stem: I won't be able to finish the report today because the data files got corrupted.

options.A: That's unfortunate. Can you recover any of the data?
options.B: You should have backed up your files.

questions.explanation: The speaker is explaining a problem preventing them from completing a task, so expressing concern and offering to problem-solve is constructive.

questions.stem: What do you think of the new campus dining options?

options.A: I think the Asian fusion station is a great addition.
options.B: The cafeteria was built three years ago.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for an opinion, so providing a specific evaluation directly answers their question.

questions.stem: I'll be right back. I need to return this book to the front desk.
questions.explanation: The speaker is leaving temporarily, so acknowledging and offering to preserve their place is a cooperative response.

audioSegments.text: Sorry I'm late. The bus broke down and I had to wait for the next one.
Do you have a pen I could borrow? Mine just ran out of ink.
I have three exams next week and I'm really stressed about it.
So, you're saying the deadline was moved to Thursday instead of Friday?
Your presentation was incredible! How did you prepare so well?
I won't be able to finish the report today because the data files got corrupted.
What do you think of the new campus dining options?
I'll be right back. I need to return this book to the front desk.
